era: 1988-1999. Heavy Grunge. E=9 -- maximum Sub Pop weight, mass and velocity combined. Georgetown. Sub Pop.
associated_musicians: Nirvana (Sub Pop peers, Jack Endino connection)

theory_nodes.concept: Sub Pop weight ceiling. TAD is heavier than Mudhoney (punk-velocity, not mass) and heavier than Nirvana (pop instincts). Mass + velocity sustained simultaneously = E=9.
theory_nodes.description: The heaviest band in the Sub Pop catalog. Physical sub-bass presence at live volume.

theory_nodes.concept: Georgetown as industrial metal origin. Duwamish River, Boeing Field, railyards. Working machinery aesthetic matches TAD's sonic approach. Cheap warehouse space enables rehearsal volume.
theory_nodes.description: Seattle's industrial south end produces the Sub Pop catalog's weight ceiling.

theory_nodes.concept: Jack Endino production partnership. TAD + Endino at Reciprocal Recording. TAD and Nirvana were Sub Pop peers in 1988-1989, both produced by Endino. Post-1991 commercial divergence is dramatic.
theory_nodes.description: Endino's drum-forward guitar-dense approach matched TAD's physical aesthetic perfectly.

cross_references.relationship: E=9. Metal summit opens. Commercial success (AIC multi-platinum) vs. critical respect without commercial breakthrough (TAD).
E drops to 7. Jack Endino appears again: produced Skin Yard as well as TAD. Proto-metal context.
Sub Pop peers 1988-1989. Both produced by Endino. The commercial distance after 1991 is one of rock history's most dramatic peer separations.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=9 + E=9 = E=18. Three simultaneous natural forces against the heaviest Sub Pop band. Metal summit opens at maximum combined energy.
